source.notes: Optimus __ MODE review  - 2026_07_09 11_29 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Ed Stockman ran a product-review call with Michael Sinkovitz of MODE (with near-silent support from Max Riemer); Michael had already tested Optimus against ZoomInfo using his old Heineken USA account and surfaced a parent-child data gap where Laredo-northbound volume was missing. Ed engaged deeply with the specific freight data, reinforced a 'same price as ZoomInfo plus shipment data' value prop, and offered a free ~20-seat trial for agent Bradley Dixon's team. The call ended with Michael owning all next steps (talk to the IBO/Agent Experience team, push Lauren and Ryan on usage) but with no firm date locked, and internal seller adoption plus corporate governance flagged as the main obstacles.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Ed was clearly responsive rather than scripted, pulling up lanes live to test Michael's Heineken hypothesis and explicitly affirming the buyer's read on parent-child routing; his acoustic profile (8.26 st pitch variation, 4.28 pace) is consistent with engaged, natural delivery.

evidence.quote: Yeah, I am. I'm finding Laredo to Oal Florida. Laredo to Oxford, North Carolina.
I think your your hypothesis is spoton around the parent entities.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could restate it in one sentence: pay the same as ZoomInfo, get comparable (95-97%) contact accuracy, plus shipment/lane data to drive new business. The accuracy claim is quantified and the price framing is concrete.

evidence.quote: I think we'll be 95 to 97% accurate with supply chain contacts
pay me what you pay them and for that you get all of this other stuff that is going to help you drive new business.

relevance.explanation: The pitch was tailored to MODE's world throughout: Ed worked the Heineken/Laredo data, framed the AI agent around MODE's own lanes and capacity, and cited BNSF and east-west rail cyclicality that directly matched Michael's Union Pacific concern.

evidence.quote: we look at your lanes, your customers, your capacity, what you guys are really good at.
the the east to west, west to east is highly cyclical and very familiar with with that problem.

discovery_progression.explanation: Ed asked sharp qualifying questions (notably tying the ZoomInfo contract end date to the renewal window) but never got that date on record and left every next step buyer-owned with no scheduled follow-up.

evidence.quote: remind me when your Zoom contract ends
let us know after you talk to that agency and we'll we'll do whatever we need to to prove some value here.

Ed Stockman.coaching_action: Before the call ends, convert your buying-signal questions into commitments: get the ZoomInfo contract end date on record and book a specific dated follow-up (e.g., a 15-minute usage review next Thursday) instead of leaving next steps open and entirely buyer-owned.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max spoke very little (~0.5 min, 3.3% of words) but his interjections showed he was tracking the account and product accurately, so there is no tone problem to penalize.

evidence.quote: Ryan's with uh he's from SUNTC, right?

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Max never articulated why the prospect should care; his airtime went to rapport and logistical asides, so there is no value statement a listener could restate.

evidence.quote: It's my father-in-law's old club. He just switched, so he gave me all of his gear.

relevance.explanation: What little Max contributed was account-specific and showed genuine familiarity with MODE's structure and entities (SUNTC, having visited the Avenger office).

evidence.quote: Yep. I've been to the Avenger office.
Ryan's with uh he's from SUNTC, right?

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked only clarifying org-structure questions and advanced nothing toward a next step; he neither owned discovery nor proposed any dated action.

evidence.quote: Ryan's with uh he's from SUNTC, right?

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Use your account familiarity to own a substantive segment of the next call - lead 2-3 discovery questions about their lanes, capacity, or adoption blockers - rather than limiting yourself to rapport and org-structure asides.

signals.signal: Tested the product hands-on before the call, using his old Heineken account as a validation case
signals.quote: from from me messing around with it because I used to be a seller

signals.signal: Unprompted positive endorsement of the tool
signals.quote: my experience with the tool is positive, guys.

signals.signal: Owns concrete internal next steps to push adoption with the decision-makers
signals.quote: you still have 79 credits. If this is compelling, get some people get some more usage from your team.

signals.signal: Objection/friction - defers timeline pending internal governance approval, the main obstacle to progress
signals.quote: Don't hold me to next week because from a corporate governance standpoint, I need to talk to our IBO experience team.
